articles.author: Collin Binkley, Alanna Durkin Richer, David Bauder
articles.description: President Donald Trump was uninjured and other top leaders of the United States were evacuated from an annual dinner of White House correspondents on Saturday night after a shooting incident outside the ballroom. One law enforcement official said a gunman had opened fire. A law enforcement officer was shot in the bullet-resistant vest but is expected to be OK, one officer told The Associated Press. The FBI said a suspect was in custody but had no details. The Secret Service and other authorities swarmed the banquet hall at the Washington Hilton as guests ducked under tables by the hundreds.
articles.title: Trump unharmed after security incident at White House correspondents' dinner

articles.author: Curtis Yee
articles.description: President Donald Trump was uninjured and evacuated from an annual dinner of White House correspondents on Saturday night after a shooting incident outside the event.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump unharmed after shooting incident outside the White House correspondents’ dinner

articles.author: Michael Biesecker, Michael Kunzelman
articles.description: Social media posts that appear to match the California man arrested in the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner show he is a highly educated tutor and amateur video game developer. A May 2025 profile photo of Cole Tomas Allen of Torrance appears to match the appearance of the man in a photo of the alleged attacker being taken into custody that was posted Saturday night by President Donald Trump. According to his online resume, the 31-year-old has degrees in mechanical engineering and computer science. For the last six years he worked for a company that offers admissions counseling and test preparation services to aspiring college students.
articles.title: Accused correspondents’ dinner attacker is tutor from California

articles.description: Incident at White House Correspondents Dinner prompts security response
articles.title: Incident at White House Correspondents Dinner prompts security response

articles.description: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner
articles.title: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner

articles.author: Munir Ahmed, Samy Magdy, Jon Gambrell
articles.description: The latest attempt at ceasefire talks between the United States and Iran has fallen flat after Tehran's top diplomat left Pakistan and President Donald Trump said he told envoys not to travel to Islamabad. The talks on Saturday were meant to follow historic face-to-face negotiations earlier this month. Iranian officials have questioned U.S. trustworthiness after U.S. forces blockaded Iranian ports in response to Tehran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz. Iran had said talks with the U.S. would be indirect. The economic fallout continues. Meanwhile, another ceasefire, between Israel and the Iranian-backed Lebanese militant Hezbollah group, was shaken as each side fired at the other.
articles.title: Trump says if Iran's leaders want to talk, 'all they have to do is call'

articles.author: Ben Finley, Konstantin Toropin, David Mchugh
articles.description: President Donald Trump says the U.S. Navy is clearing Iranian mines from the Strait of Hormuz. The vital sea route for Persian Gulf oil is closed to most ships, and that's a strain on the global economy. Experts say sweeping for underwater explosives could take months despite a tenuous ceasefire between the United States and Iran in the war. Any future claims that the U.S. cleared the waterway where 20% of the world’s oil typically passes might fail to convince commercial freighters and their insurers that it's finally safe.
articles.title: US says it's clearing Iranian mines in effort to open the Strait of Hormuz

articles.author: Sam Metz
articles.description: The post-Oct. 7 order in the Middle East is barely pieced together by conditional ceasefires and mutual threats. Israel has inflicted heavy blows on Iran and its allies Hezbollah and Hamas, but it has yet to vanquish them and is still regularly striking in Lebanon and Gaza. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is under mounting pressure to deliver a clear victory ahead of elections later this year. U.S. President Donald Trump still appears to be seeking a nuclear deal with Iran and wider peace in the Middle East. But ceasefire talks have so far produced little and the two countries are locked in an escalating standoff over the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.title: Trump has reached shaky ceasefires in Iran, Lebanon and Gaza but major issues are unresolved

articles.author: Sam Metz, Wafaa Shurafa
articles.description: Palestinians have voted in local elections, the first in two decades in Gaza and the first in the Israeli occupied West Bank since the start of the war in Gaza. The Palestinian Authority promoted Saturday's local races following reforms it enacted last year following demands from international backers. Election officials said the vote in Gaza’s Deir al-Balah was a largely symbolic “pilot" and part of the PA's effort to politically link the territories. Hamas didn't field candidates or prevent the vote from taking place. Analysts say the lack of candidates and party participation reflects political disillusionment under the authority’s aging leadership.
articles.title: Palestinians vote in first Gaza election in over 20 years

articles.author: Melanie Lidman
articles.description: Palestinians have buried a pregnant woman and her two children killed in Israeli strikes in Gaza. At least 13 people died in the attacks on Friday, including two men in Gaza City and eight people in Khan Younis. Officials said the eight, including four police officers, died when Israel targeted a police vehicle. Khalid Al-Tanani lost his wife and two children in the strikes. Israel's military said militants threatened troops, prompting the airstrike. Despite a ceasefire, Israeli strikes continue to disrupt the truce. Gaza’s Health Ministry reports over 790 deaths since the ceasefire began six months ago.
articles.title: Gaza mourns pregnant woman and children killed in Israeli strikes
